title: AI Risk Assessment Checklist
description: Comprehensive checklist for conducting AI risk assessments across the system lifecycle. Items organized by assessment phase with AI-specific considerations.

phases.description: Risk assessment activities before AI system development begins.

items.title: Verify training data provenance and legal basis
items.description: Confirm all training data sources are documented with clear provenance, legal basis for collection, and appropriate consent or licensing.

items.title: Assess training data representativeness and bias
items.description: Evaluate whether training data is representative of the target population and test for demographic biases that could lead to discriminatory outputs.

items.title: Classify AI system risk tier
items.description: Determine the AI system's risk classification per EU AI Act tiers (prohibited, high-risk, limited, minimal) and organizational risk taxonomy.

items.title: Conduct Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment
items.description: For high-risk AI systems, complete FRIA covering all potentially affected fundamental rights with proportionality and necessity assessment.

phases.description: Risk assessment activities during AI model development and training.

items.title: Evaluate model bias metrics
items.description: Measure fairness metrics (demographic parity, equalized odds, calibration) across all protected characteristics relevant to the use case.

items.title: Assess explainability requirements
items.description: Determine required explainability level proportionate to risk tier and implement appropriate methods (SHAP, LIME, attention maps, counterfactuals).

items.title: Verify human oversight mechanisms
items.description: Confirm human oversight design is proportionate to risk level: human-in-the-loop for high-risk, human-on-the-loop for medium-risk, with kill switches tested.

items.title: Conduct adversarial robustness testing
items.description: Test model resilience against adversarial inputs, evasion attacks, and edge cases. For generative AI, include prompt injection and jailbreak testing.

items.title: Complete model documentation (model card)
items.description: Document model purpose, architecture, training methodology, performance metrics, known limitations, intended use, and out-of-scope uses.

phases.description: Risk assessment activities before AI system goes to production.

items.title: Review data protection impact assessment
items.description: Verify DPIA is completed and approved by DPO for systems processing personal data, covering model memorization and inference-time data risks.

items.title: Validate deployment readiness checklist
items.description: Confirm all pre-deployment prerequisites are met: testing complete, documentation current, approvals obtained, monitoring configured.

items.title: Verify operator training completion
items.description: Confirm all human operators have completed training covering AI system capabilities, limitations, failure modes, and override procedures.

items.title: Confirm AI disclosure and transparency notices
items.description: Verify user-facing transparency notices are in place disclosing AI involvement in decisions, with explanations accessible in plain language.

phases.description: Ongoing risk assessment activities after AI system is in production.

items.title: Monitor model performance and drift
items.description: Verify continuous monitoring is active for data drift, concept drift, accuracy degradation, and fairness metric changes with defined alert thresholds.

items.title: Review AI incident response readiness
items.description: Confirm incident response plan covers AI-specific scenarios, regulatory notification timelines are understood, and rollback capability is verified.

items.title: Assess override and intervention patterns
items.description: Analyze human override rates and patterns to detect automation bias (low override rates) or systematic model failures (high override rates).

items.title: Conduct periodic risk reassessment
items.description: Reassess AI system risks at defined intervals or when triggered by material changes (model update, scope expansion, new regulations, incident).

items.title: Evaluate societal and environmental impact
items.description: Review actual societal impact against FRIA predictions. Track environmental metrics (energy, carbon) and workforce effects. Update impact assessments as needed.
